New Perplexity AI super agent platform is insane. Today I'm going to show you something that just dropped and it's wild. Perplexity just built a whole new platform for AI agents. One API that does everything. Search models, embeddings, agents, all of it. This could replace your entire AI stack. And I'm going to break down exactly how it works right now. So, Perplexi just made a massive move. And nobody is really talking about this yet. They went from being an AI search engine to becoming a full platform where you can build AI agents like actual autonomous agents that go out, search the web, pull data, think through problems, and complete entire workflows on their own all through one single API. Now, why should you care about this? Let me explain. If you've ever tried to build anything with AI before, you know it's a mess. You need one service for your AI model. You need another service for search. You need another one for embeddings. you need something else to tie it all together. It's like having four different remotes for one TV. It's annoying. It's complicated and it takes forever to set up. That's the problem Perplexity just solved. They put everything into one place. One API, one platform, done. And I'm going to walk you through every single piece of it because this is genuinely one of the biggest shifts I've seen in the AI space this year. Stick around because what I show you at the end is going to blow your mind. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you comment below. All right, so let's get into it. The new Perplexity API platform has four main parts, and each one is powerful on its own, but together they're a beast. Number one is the agent API. This is the big one. This lets you build AR agents that can search the web, read documents, reason through problems, use tools, and complete multi-step tasks. So, imagine you tell an agent to research a topic, find 10 sources, summarize them, and then write a full report. It just does it all by itself. No handholding, no switching between tools. One agent handles the whole thing from start to finish. And here's what makes it even crazier. The platform is model agnostic. That means you're not locked into one AI model. You can use models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, XAI, Nvidia all through the same API. So if you want to use one model for research and a different model for writing, you can do that all inside one system. That's huge. Number two is the search API. Perplexity opened up the same search engine that powers their main product. So now any app or agent you build can pull real-time data from the internet. Fresh results filtered by domain, multiple queries at once. This is the same tech that makes Perplexity so good at answering questions. And now developers can plug it into anything. Number three is the embeddings API. Now this one is more technical, but stay with me because it matters. Embeddings let AI understand the meaning behind text. So if you're building something that needs to search through documents or remember past conversations or find similar content, this is what powers that. Perplexity released their own embedding models that are built for large scale stuff. So if you're working with tons of data, this handles it. Number four is the sandbox API. This one is coming soon, but when it drops, agents will be able to run code, test things, and execute tasks in a safe environment. Think of it like giving your AI agent a computer to work on. It can try things out without breaking anything. That's next level. Now, let me show you why this actually changes the game. Before this update, if you wanted to build an AI agent, here's what your setup looked like. You needed an AI model provider like OpenAI or Anthropic. You needed a search API like Serpay. You needed an embedding service like Pine Cone. You needed an orchestration tool like Langchain. And you needed custom infrastructure to glue it all together. Five different services, five different accounts, five different sets of documentation. It was a nightmare. Now with Plexity, that whole stack collapses into one platform. One API call gives you the model, the search, the embeddings, and the agent logic. That's it. Done. Think about how much simpler that makes everything. Now, real quick. If you're someone who wants to use AI to scale your business, get more customers, and automate your workflows with tools like Perplexity's new agent platform, you need to check out the AI profit boardroom. It's the best place to learn how to actually put these AI tools to work in your business. I'll drop the link in the description and comments below. All right, let me show you some real use cases because this is where it gets fun. Use case one, AI research agent. You give it the topic, it goes out, searches the web, finds sources, reads through them, summarizes everything, and writes you a full report. What used to take you hours now takes minutes, and it's pulling real-time data, so it's always up to date. Use case two, startup analyst agent. Say you want to know what's happening in the AI startup world. You tell the agent to find the top startups launched in the last year, compare what they're building, look at their tech, and generate a market overview. It handles all of that automatically. Use case three, content creator agent. This one is for people like me. You tell the agent to research trending AI news this week, come up with video ideas, and write scripts for each one. That's an entire content pipeline handled by one agent. And because it uses real-time search, it's always working with fresh information, not outdated training data. Use case for coding agent. You tell it to build a landing page with specific features. Tailwind CSS the works and it generates the full code not just snippets the whole thing ready to use. Now here's the bigger picture and this is important. The AI industry is moving from chat bots to agents. Chat bots wait for you to ask a question. Agents go out and do things on their own. That's a massive shift. And Perplexity just positioned itself right at the center of it. They're not just competing with Google for search anymore. They're going after Open AAI Anthropic and Google as an AI platform. They want to be the place where developers build their AI agents. And honestly, with what they just launched, they have a real shot at it. The agents also come with built-in tools, web search, URL fetching, and different reasoning modes. You can set your agent to do a quick search, a deep research dive, or an advanced deep research session, depending on what you need. And you can control the token so your agent doesn't go off the rails. It's all built in. No extra setup. Perplexity is also pushing hard into the enterprise space. They're building agent systems for businesses that need to automate complex workflows. Think customer research, market analysis, competitive intelligence, all running on AI agents. This isn't just for developers tinkering around. This is for real business use. And that's what I think makes this update so significant. It's not just a cool new feature. It's Perplexity saying we're not just a search engine anymore. We're building the operating system for AI agents. If AI agents are the future and everyone in the industry seems to agree that they are, then the platform where those agents get built is going to be incredibly important. Think about it like this. AWS became the backbone of the internet. Every app, every website, every startup ran on AWS. Perplexity is trying to do the same thing for AI agents. They want to be the infrastructure layer that everything runs on. And with this update, they just took a massive step in that direction. So, here's what I want you to do.
